candidate_problems.text: How can 'control structure' or 'functional invariant' be made precise enough to distinguish genuinely knowledge-generating analogies from merely suggestive redescriptions? The conjecture depends on a nontrivial criterion of structural sameness across domains, but it leaves open what counts as preservation, how much mismatch is tolerable, and how to test whether a purported invariant is truly load-bearing rather than retrospectively imposed.

What is the epistemic mechanism by which an analogy yields genuinely new target knowledge rather than only heuristic guidance? The conjecture says novelty arises when shared invariants imply new consequences in the target, but it remains unresolved how those consequences were not already implicit in prior target knowledge, and what separates real discovery from a change in representation, attention, or search efficiency.

Under what conditions does successful cross-domain analogy justify ontological commitment to a real abstract organizational kind, rather than only a useful model class? The conjecture moves from explanatory success to 'ontology discovery,' but this raises an open question about when recurring control architectures track something objectively real in the world versus when they are just human-selected compressions that travel well across domains.
